guards.description: Riverpod annotation, generated part, and notifier contract.
Render-only UI boundaries for screens, widgets, and shared UI.
go_router contract and navigation API boundaries.
No hardcoded user-facing literals in main/presentation UI.
Feature screens must rely on theme surface/background tokens.
State ownership rules for providers, DI, and shared controllers.
Shared render layers stay provider-agnostic and receive state from above.
Shared widget layers follow Memora primitive/composite/layout/screen boundaries.
Feature UI and shared shells consume the centralized theme foundation.
Feature UI and shared shells must not inline raw UI constants that belong in core config or theme tokens.
Inline numeric UI literals must route through tokens, theme extensions, or semantic named constants.
Local presentation constants must not duplicate theme/token/l10n ownership.
Feature UI and shared shells consume spacing through context tokens instead of raw numeric spacing.
Feature UI should consume shared design-system widgets instead of raw Material counterparts.
Basic code quality guard for presentation and theme layers.
Accessibility baseline for tappable controls in presentation layers.
Alpha values must flow through opacity tokens.
GoRouter navigation must use generated typed route data instead of string-based locations.
Data transport models and Retrofit bodies must use generated serialization.
Transport parsers must bind a single canonical backend key per field.
